Top notch lawyer and team who strive for excellence and deliver results. I highly recommend this firm for VA disability claims/appeals and appreciate their hard work and dedication to their important work.

I can’t thank Mr. Rousseau and his team enough for their years of dedication and support. They treated me with respect and truly believed in my case when others did not. I had very limited documentation from my deployment, and many incidents occurred under conditions where record-keeping was difficult, yet they never gave up on me. Their hard work led to an outcome that changed my life—and my family’s life—for the better. I am deeply grateful and highly recommend Mr. Rousseau and his team to any veteran seeking someone who genuinely cares and will fight for them.

Fantastic representation by Rousseau Legal Services. Rick and his entire staff make you feel like you are their only client. The personal attention to your case and their clear communication make the complicated process easy to understand.
Took my request immediately and contacted me back within 24 hours.

Mr. Rousseau and his staff were amazing! They worked tirelessly for months (maybe closer to two years) to help me obtain my VA disability rating. They stayed in touch regularly, and I was never left guessing about the process as it worked through both the claim and the appeals process. I highly recommend Mr. Rousseau's office as legal representation.

Criss & Rousseau are examples of the finest service that lawyers can provide. Rick Rousseau helped me claim an adjustment to my VA disability determination. He and his team did such a good job that the VA granted the adjustment on the very first try. I heartily recommend using them if you have the need. I will refer friends and colleagues to them for sure!

I struggled with the VA for approximately 15 years in an attempt to have them recognize active duty time spent with the USMC. Endless hours. Endless effort in providing actual USMC documentation to the VA. All efforts were in vain. I went to VSO's in many states seeking assistance. All to no avail.

As a final resort I made contact with Mr. Rousseau an attorney in Texas. He happened to be a retired 08 (ARMY). Spoke with him and explained my plight. Explained to him my situation and expressed to him that I had come to believe that  my case represented a  classic case of a Clear & Unmistakable Error(s) situation. A CUE if ever one existed. Since CUE cases are rarely ruled in favor of the claimants (10% is the average success rate) he heard me out and agreed to take my case regardless of this reality.

He delved deeply into his search for all available records available in the VA bureaucracy. He established the facts. He presented the case and claim in a very short time period.

HE & HIS STAFF WON. The claim was upheld and and adjudicated in my favor.

This resulted in my receiving a back pay settlement in excess of $230,000.

I shall remain forever grateful.

Ed Bell USN/USMC

Rick is the best! His staff is amazing! Responsive, professional, compassionate, pays attention to details and follows through.

I highly recommend!

The Criss & Rousseau firm took care of me from the beginning to the end of my case. Anytime I had questions, they were answered promptly! I was made to feel included and was always made aware of any changes that happened with my case. I will forever be grateful to this highly professional and dedicated team!!

They are AMAZING! Walked me through my VA Claim process from beginning to end. Very responsive and personalized service. I have and will continue to recommend this firm to all my Veteran Brothers and Sisters looking to claim their VA Benefits.

I am deeply grateful to Mr. Rousseau and his staff for their dedication and support in handling my case. They took on my VA case when other law firms wouldn't and worked tirelessly to secure a successful outcome for me. Thank you so much !!

I've been working with these folks for a while now and they are very professional, considerate, do everything in a timely manner and very on top of what they're doing. I couldn't recommend anyone better, because there isn't anyone.

Final update: After 2 years, my claim was actually approved! I'm surprised to say the least but this is a testament to Mr. Rousseau' determination and persistence. And thank you to all his staff that answered promptly, called me back, and responded to emails quickly! See below for the original review:

Mr. Rousseau was very easy to talk to and after hearing my plea, he agreed to take my case. We spent about a year gathering everything together. Unfortunately, the case did not win but I was still thoroughly satisfied with the service he provided, the questions he answered, and his availability throughout the process.

Update 1: Mr. Rousseau continues to fight for my case after more than a year. At one point I had thought it was simply over but we're at the final stages. Very satisfied with his service.
Absolutely great service from Miss McKayla. I'm sure her compassion and care has gotten this office many clients.
Definitely recommend! Mr Rousseau always kept in touch with my case, and was honest about how long it would take from the start. Most importantly, he got it done for me. Nothing but praise.

I can't say enough good things about this law firm. I have been working with Rick for over 5 years now. The fact that he has stuck with me this entire time is a testament to his desire to help. And I would be lost in the system without his guidance and perseverance. I trust him completely. And you should too.
Helped with my VA claim, did what I could not. Thank you so much.

I have worked with Rick and his team for several years now on a case against the government.  They have been fabulously helpful throughout the process and continue to assist with everything needed.  Since this case is taking years, I would have given up (which is what the government wants) but the team has kept us on track and continues to receive positive judgements on our behalf!

After wasting a couple years seeking seeking compensation from the Veterans Admin for injuries sustained during active duty, and getting nowhere, a friend recommended I make a free consultation with Mr. Rousseau. This was without a doubt one of the best decisions I ever made in my life. Although it took 2 1/2 years, mostly because of the Covid shutdown, Mr. Rousseau and his legal team never gave up and fought battle after battle with them to win my case. They have my utmost respect and appreciation for what they did. I will be forever grateful.

reviews.text:
This review documents the ongoing pattern of inaction, lack of urgency, and failure to meet basic professional standards exhibited by Nancy throughout her involvement in my case. Her conduct significantly harmed my ability to protect my rights and my daughter’s well-being during a highly sensitive and urgent legal process.

⸻

1. Failure to Act During an Emergency

I approached Nancy desperate and in an emergency situation involving my divorce and my daughter’s safety. I made it clear that immediate legal intervention was needed.
Despite the urgency, Nancy took no meaningful action to address the situation or to move the case forward.

⸻

2. Ignoring Violations of the Temporary Orders

I repeatedly informed Nancy about multiple violations of the Temporary Orders, providing details and evidence.
She failed to:
•	file any motions,
•	advise me on immediate remedies,
•	notify the court,
•	or take any steps to enforce the orders or protect my parental rights.

Her lack of response left me vulnerable during moments that required urgent legal action.

⸻

3. Failure to Present Key Evidence in Court

One of the most serious issues was that Nancy did not present crucial evidence in court, evidence that was necessary to support my claims, demonstrate the violations, and show the pattern of behavior affecting my child and this case.
This omission directly weakened my position and affected the court’s ability to fully understand the facts.

⸻

4. Lack of Follow-Through and Progress

Throughout her time on my case, Nancy did not prepare essential filings, did not follow up on critical issues, and did not provide a timeline or structure for how we would proceed. The case stagnated under her management.

⸻

5. Poor Communication

Nancy consistently failed to respond promptly to messages—sometimes not responding at all—especially when the matters were time-sensitive or related to court deadlines. This forced me to handle complicated legal issues without proper support.

⸻

6. No Strategy or Guidance

Despite my requests for direction, she did not offer a clear strategy, actionable advice, or explanation of the steps needed in my case. I was left without proper representation during a complex legal process.

⸻

7. Withdrawal Only After Being Confronted

It was only after I confronted her about her inaction and lack of results that she expressed the desire to withdraw from my case. This timing demonstrates avoidance of accountability rather than a professional decision made in my best interest.

⸻

8. Harm Caused to My Case

Because of her negligence:
•	evidence was not presented,
•	violations were ignored,
•	deadlines were missed or delayed,
•	and my legal position was weakened unnecessarily.

Her inaction caused avoidable stress, confusion, and disadvantage at critical moments.

⸻

Conclusion

Nancy’s failure to act during an emergency, her refusal to address violations of the Temporary Orders, and her omission of key evidence in court represent serious breaches of professional duty.

This review is submitted to document the harm caused by her inaction and to ensure that future representation meets the standard required to protect my rights and my daughter’s well-being.

I don’t have enough words to express regarding the dedication and professionalism of Nancy and Anna! This dynamic team supported me faithfully throughout my divorce, which involved custody of my two year old son. Through our partnership Nancy was able to secure me a 50/50 possession schedule and even better terms regarding support of my son.

Nancy and Anna were both always punctual with responding to motions or any other legal matters that transpired in this case. They were always available via email, text message and phone call. They even offered great rates and various payment arrangements if needed.

Nancy is one who will give you her honest opinion once she has analyzed all details of your case. She is pretty spot on as it relates to outcomes, as she has stated to me before that her knowledge is a result of her ”years of practice”. Trust the wisdom she shares.

I know custody can be a daunting thought and undertaking for men, but if you stay engaged with your children, document, be a positive co-parent, help out financially whenever you can, the courts will see that and they will in many ways honor your dedication to your child(ren).

Special thanks to this wonderful team!
-Richard

reviews.text:
A deep heartfelt thank you to Nancy and her Team (Minnie, Anna and Gloria) for assisting me in my divorce process. After being married for 17 years to a military member, having 5 children and owning a home together, searching for an attorney that was experienced and compassionate was of utmost importance  to me and what Nancy certainly brought to the table.

Here’s my Timeline…..
- 4/24/24: Retained Nancy for Divorce
- 5/2/24: Divorce Petition Filed
- 5/17/24: attempted to reconcile and put a pause on divorce process
- 8/8/24: Proceeded with Divorce process
- 8/26/24: Informal Settlement Conference
- 10/28/24: Judge signed Divorce Decree

In 6 months, Nancy and her team provided swift communication, answered my questions thoroughly, and guided my decision making process all while maintaining a supportive client attorney relationship.

I highly recommend Nancy Eaton and her associates.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks, Katrina! We're very happy that things went so smoothly. And you got a nice check of unused retainer back! ~Nancy

reviews.text: I’ve been a loyal client of Nancy's for several years, and while the rest of the team has always been friendly and professional, interactions with the receptionist, Minnie, have almost always been unpleasant. She’s rude, short, dismissive, and treats clients as if they’re an inconvenience to her, which creates a really negative experience. I've referred other people who feel the same way and have mentioned her attitude as a reason they hesitate meet with Nancy. I can 100% confirm this. It’s unfortunate because this office has high standards, but Minnie’s lack of courtesy and empathy really undermines that. I hope the management can address this, as it’s the one thing that truly stands out as a problem.
reviews.ownerResponse: Since you used a fake name, I can't really address the issue since I don't know if you are an actual client. Feel free to email me. ~Nancy

reviews.text: Treats me with with kindness and respect. Always keeps me grounded and level headed and takes the stress off of me. Mr. Smith is professional and kind.
Very caring and cares for his clients.

Mr. Smith was hired on under false promises. He promised he would not be overloaded but every time I talked to him he has claimed to be so busy with other cases he could not call me back till weeks or months later sometimes.after being asked to provide a copy of our contract he would tell me no. He claimed hes done an investigation but could not find reports I requested that would prove exculpatory, even going to the lengths as saying its probably all in your head. After only one week of being out I was able to pull up the very same reports using the very same info provided to him showing his negligence. When asked to file for a bond reduction he would not cited the case law pertaining to my case under txccp art.17.151 release because of delay which would fall under ex parte jones 803 s.w. 2d 718. Smith would constantly degrade me as a defendant and as a human being claiming "criminals are dumb I'm the one with a law degree". Smith made sure for the hearing A) I was not present and B) I was restricted out of the county over 200 miles away so to not be able to contact witnesses or check on my own property income. Smith has not contacted a single witness in my case or challenged lack of evidence to prove probable cause. He has not gone over any of the evidence with me except for a statement. When asked of his investigators name he will not provide it and when I hired my own investigator he will not allow him to see all of the evidence. He has kept me from all hearings which is a violation of txccp art 28.01 (pretrial) and has done hisdispicable duty to prevent me from having a fair trial.  On his motion to withdraw from my case he claimed I did not pay him, I had proof via text message he had the check and I only asked him to wait a few days to deposit it due to financial troubles I had that he knew of. Then he filed an addendum to the motion that day saying the relationship was not working. I was not allowed to talk or elaborate of course so he was granted permission to withdraw with out a proffer of facts and my request for a grievance or my money paid to him back was not granted. He even gave me the wrong time for the hearing hoping I would not show up, but of course he believes text messages are proper form of notice even though they have the wrong info in them. Mr. Smith is deceitful and will tell you or your family anything to get your money, his knowledge of the law has proved under par and only relies on his relationship with the prosecutor or judicial members to make a case. I have proof of all statements and none of what I state in this article is based on biased opinion.
reviews.ownerResponse: I'm sorry Mr Brickley, but after you grossly betrayed my trust, our attorney client relationship was ruined.  I hope one day you can accept responsibility for your situation.  Good luck sir.

reviews.ownerResponse: I am sorry you were not satisfied with your legal consultation. While I cannot change your feelings, I do need to clarify a few items. I spoke with you on multiple occasions and reviewed all the legal documentation you provided. After review, we discussed in some length all the legal options as well as practical solutions. In some instances, there are really no good legal avenues to remedy a situation. I am sorry the real estate situation you are in makes you feel “dumb and ripped off”. If you feel the consultation was of no benefit, please contact my office and they will refund the $175.00 consultation fee.

reviews.text: I've had a couple different attorney's, and the way I felt like family. I highly recommend,  if you're looking for a disability attorney, give them a call. Thank you all!!!

Both of the Alcozer (father and son) were amazing. They assisted in navigating the process, were very responsive and helpful. Mr Alcozer expressed to the judge things I couldn't express on my own. Attained the Law Office of Michalk Beatty & Alcozer in 2023, and he and his staff have been very Professional, Informative, and caring about my situation and concerns. They are working my case intensely, and keeping me updated on progress. I HIGHLY recommend this Law Firm if your ever in need of an,  Knowledgeable, Professional, and Caring Law Firm. Thank You.

If I could deliver negative stars, I would.
I was referred to Jay Beatty via the State Bar of Texas. I had been searching for a consultation to understand better how to handle being sued for a debt. I am no lawyer; I have no formal legal education (hence seeking advice from someone who knew what they were talking about). I paid $20 in hopes of gaining some clarity on what my next steps should be. Within minutes of our consultation, Jay was off-putting. I had problems with my home printer and opted to bring my documents on my laptop. Jay, unable to operate a laptop, asked if I could email him the documents. I gladly sent them. After leaving the room to print said documents, Jay returns with a snarky remark, "Funny how that works." Okay... I let that go. Delving further into the issue at hand, Jay realizes there is no defense. I'm not seeking a way out of my responsibilities; I am seeking advice on approaching them. I ask how am I supposed to write an answer to the court: fish out of water here. His response is, "Well, I can't show you without doing it for you."
This blatant condescending tone and rude demeanor continued maybe 10 minutes before I walked out. I quickly realized this man didn't see a profitable client, which meant I wasn't entitled to be spoken to with respect or given the time of day to offer a bit of guidance.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We are participants in the State Bar referral service. Pursuant to the State Bar, the $20.00 consultation is for up to 30 minutes and is meant to allow the attorney to determine whether they are able to assist with the person’s case or not. The attorney is not obligated to accept the case, or even provide legal advice during this initial review.
In your case, during the 45 minutes I met with you, not only did we discuss your legal matter but went over the various options you had to respond to your situation. You were instructed that in your case it did not make financial sense to retain our firm. You were advised you needed to file an answer to the lawsuit or a judgment would be taken against you. At that juncture, you angrily got upset that I did not ‘show you how to do the answer.’
In order to rectify any misunderstanding, we are certainly willing to refund you the $20.00 you paid for the consultation.

My review is based off of my experience walking in and having a consultation.

Just seen a commercial and it reminded me of my experience when I was trying to obtain some help from them in the past. The commercial acts like they care about the community but I did not get that vibe when I went into the building. Met with them they spoke with my son and me and then told us it would be about $10,000 just to get started, that grandparents didn't have rights and I get that but they just acted like they didn't want the case and it couldn't be done. I got the feeling that our issue wasn't that important and kinda felt like they just didn't want the business cuz it wouldn't a very profitable case for them. Upon asking around a few people about lawyers in Bell county I was able to find a lawyer that helped with no problem and only charged $4,000. Got the joint custody for my grandbaby that this place said wouldn't likely happen, saved a ton and very happy that I didn't end up using this firm. Do research. Just because they got a nice building doesn't mean they actually care.

Everyone grows up listening to the importance of life insurance.  However, there is another document which receives little attention, and this is a Living Trust. When my sister passed away, she didn’t have a will or written directive and I was her only family.  Although she had no real property or little funds, I was still left with the process of traveling from my out of state home to research, inventory and probating her estate which was time consuming, and I had out of pocket expenses.  I promised myself this would not be the case for me. A friend recommended Silverblatt law offices and my husband, and I made an appointment. I was not sure what to expect but was treated by the staff and Mr. Silverblatt as if we were the only clients for the day.   He discussed with us our goals for the trust and made some suggestions which were helpful, and we proceeded with the trust. When a few years later there was a change in the family dynamic, we returned, and it was updated and will continue to make changes as needed.  The most important objective of a trust is to give you peace of mind much the same as life insurance.  Ensuring your wishes for your estates distributions is clear and your loved ones have no doubts your intentions.
reviews.ownerResponse: I am so grateful!

reviews.text: "I don't usually take the time to write reviews on things, but if you are seeking an immigration attorney, then you probably feel that your decision could be one of the most important decisions in your life. My husband is a US citizen, and I  was here with a tourist visa .We didn’t know anything about the process , and we needed an expert. We shopped around for months, looking at the killeen Lawyers list, other publications that ranked lawyers, etc. We ask a few of these lawyers about our case, and while I'm sure most of them would have done a good job, they also charged an extremely high rate . We found EXCELLENT reviews from the people she has actually helped with their immigration issues. We met with Jasmine and she immediately gave us some great information about our case. My husband doesn’t speak Spanish and my first language is Spanish and Jasmine was Awesome helping both of us with English and Spanish .She was very nice and we felt a lot better meeting with her than the other lawyers we had met with. Jasmin Rios and her staff took our case, and even went the extra mile when she gave us different payment methods . She coached us for our interview and was present for it as well. I can't tell you how happy I am  to finally have legal status. We owe it all to RIOS LAW GROUP . Trust me, if you need an immigration attorney, you can't go wrong with her team, and for an extremely fair price compared to his peers. Jasmine Rios , thank you, from both of us. I hope this review will help others with their decision."

I’d like to take a moment to speak on how incredibly thankful I am to Mr. Williamson and his entire team. Mr. Williamson took on my case at the end of 2023. Prior to our first meeting I was very uncertain because of the perplexity of my case, I knew that in order to get the best possible outcome I’d have to obtain an attorney whom cared about the future of my children the way I do. Mr. Williamson exceeded those expectations, no calls went unanswered, no email went unread, and every time I had any questions or a better understanding about what was going on in court (court terms) he and his team were right there to assist. Mr. Williamson is extremely knowledgeable and informative, I’d recommend him in a heartbeat.
Quick Response to emails, calls etc. Understanding to our roller coaster emotions & respectful to our opinions.

I went into mediation with hope that it would be a fair and balanced process. Unfortunately, that was far from the reality of what transpired. My attorney and I were placed in a separate room from the opposing party and their lawyer, which is standard practice. However, for more than half of the five-hour session, the mediator spent the majority of the time with the opposing side. When he finally returned to present a counteroffer, it became immediately clear that the terms were not in the best interest of my special needs child.

As he began speaking, I attempted to interject, knowing that what was being proposed was unacceptable. Instead of facilitating a discussion, as a mediator should, he insisted I let him finish, only to then overtalk me when I disagreed. His response? Calling me "unreasonable" and displaying a blatant misogynistic attitude. His demeanor was not neutral or professional—he was dismissive, rude, and aggressive.

For a process meant to be about compromise and fairness, this experience was nothing short of a slap in the face. The mediator’s behavior was so appalling that he eventually had to call my lawyer to continue discussions because I no longer felt comfortable engaging with him. At times, it truly felt as though he was advocating for the opposing side rather than mediating.

This experience was unacceptable, and I will be filing a formal complaint beyond this review. No one should have to endure this type of unprofessionalism, especially in sensitive matters like those involving children. I can only hope that others are spared from encountering this kind of treatment.
Worth every penny! Got it done. No BS!
Attorney that fight for your rights. He’s an awesome attorney. I will definitely use him again

I contacted Mr. Williamson’s office in 2022, after discharging attorney Brett Pritchard from my case. I was totally distressed and broke after Mr. Pritchard billed me for an entire year close to the amount of $30,000. This was for a marriage that lasted almost three years and the “only” property we had to divide was a house. When I departed Mr. Pritchard I was still married.
Mr. Williamson took my case for a very fair & reasonable price. He did not send me erroneous bills just to take my money. He did represent me with the utmost professionalism! He fought for me from the beginning to the end. Mr. Williamson is extremely knowledgeable and he truly cares about his clients. Everyone in the office is very kind and they listen to your needs. The property division (home) was just finalized & I am overjoyed with the outcome of my case! Had I stayed with Brett Pritchard, I’d probably be homeless by now. Thank you Mr. Williamson!

The OAG will not release information to me until this letter is sent. reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for your review and feedback. I am sorry that you are frustrated and felt the need to leave such a review. Unfortunately, due to confidentiality reasons, I cannot fully respond to your claims made herein. I can say, however, that had you taken the time to gather all the facts, then you would see how your review is completely inaccurate on all accounts.

Since I cannot go into too much detail, I would just reference you to your Clio file and messages where you will find that your statement above is inaccurate and that you need to take your frustrations out on a different office.

Regarding your phone call, I am sorry that you felt that my front desk staff was rude and unprofessional. However, my office is right next to the front desk and I overheard the entirety of our end of the conversation when you called. At no point did I believe that he was rude or unprofessional to you. He immediately came to my office to let me know what was going on, at which point I attempted to reach out to you to discuss and let you know that you were misinformed by somebody not associated with my office.

Again, I am sorry that your frustration led you to leave a factually inaccurate review. Hopefully you will get your situation resolved, but I can assure you that the problem is not at this end. Thank you.

I was told there wasn't enough money in the case and they charge 39% furthermore I engaged another law firm we settled for 2.5 million.
Avoid these folks who think you must get runover by a semi truck etc..why? Very easy lawsuit that pays very well.
reviews.ownerResponse: Richard, I am sorry to hear you had an unpleasant experience with our team. Unfortunately, we cannot accept every case – as much as we wish we could! We strive to keep open communication, so if you have any questions or concerns, please contact our office. While we may not be able to accept your case, we would be happy to guide you towards your next steps. Thank you, Richard!

reviews.text: My name is Sandra. In November of 2022 | was involved in a no fault collision. I had back injuries and for almost 3 years endured several surgeries and long therapy sessions. Through all this the firm of Daniel Stark (personal injury lawyers) stood with me. Team leader Myranda, along with Kallyn and a host of staff members kept me informed of every step made in my case. They answered every question and addressed every concern I had. I truly felt like a person and not just a case, ( who knew lawyers cared)? On one of our many phone calls Myranda found out that I was having a difficult time moving and lifting heavy things, like groceries from my car. She had a wheeled shopping cart delivered to my home; on my birthday I received flowers, when my mother passed a sympathy bouquet arrived. I was never lied to or misled about anything concerning my case. The entire experience was made as stress free as was possible. It took these professionals time and planning to bring my case to a satisfactory conclusion, but I would do it all over again with this firm. Thank you Myranda, Kallyn and the rest of the team.
reviews.ownerResponse: Sandra, thank you for sharing your experience. We're delighted to hear that Myranda, Kallyn, and the team stood by you through such a difficult time, not only offering professionalism and expertise but also compassion and sympathy. We sincerely hope you never need us again, but if you do, we will be here to fiercely advocate for you!

I reached out again to Miss Biebas to see if she could help me since she told me to wait a year and it’s already been a year, but out of pettiness since I decided to reach out to another lawyer, they are refusing to help. I only asked for help from another lawyer because my kids needed help immediately and Mrs Biebas was not willing to help. Save your money and any mom’s out there please do not hire this attorney. reviews.text:
I was court-appointed this office for a criminal case and was extremely nervous. I wanted to make sure my case was taken seriously and that I had the opportunity to fight it. When I called, I spoke with Alex, the legal assistant, who came across as completely rude and dismissive. I even had to ask if I could explain my situation since my arraignment was coming up so quickly.

She told me I would hear something once the lawyers spoke with other lawyers, but there was no reassurance, no compassion, and no guidance. I even said I’d be willing to pay just to get some actual help or to speak to the attorney himself, yet I still felt like my life and circumstances didn’t matter.

I am not a criminal, nor do I want to be treated like one. I’m a full-time working mom and a military spouse, and even sharing that didn’t seem to make any difference in how I was treated.

Because of this, the very next day I paid $6k for a different attorney and told this office to turn over my documents. I would never recommend relying on this office if you want someone to truly care about your case.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share your experience with our firm. While we disagree with the events as you have outlined, we understand that our clients, and their loved ones, are going through a difficult time that brings them to our office. We know that dealing with a court matter has a high level of uncertainty and periods of no development or change in status, which only heightens the difficulty and frustration. This is why we take the time for our clients. We answer their phone calls and emails. We handle every case with empathy and kindness.

We are glad that you have found legal counsel, and wish you the best on your outcome.

Her defense was on point and she presented a strong argument for any attack by the prosecutor. I was impressed by her prowess and watching her in action in the court room. She was direct and honest about all options and her expert advice always payed off. In the end, as a convicted felon and repeat offender. I walked away with 2 dismissals and facing 2-20 year sentence with only 30 days in county jail. reviews.text: This firm is amazing. They worked with me on payments, stayed on top of my case and helped calm me down. Wonderful people and a great attorney.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks so much, Aura. So glad we got the best result in your case!

reviews.text:
I retained Mrs. Harrell as my attorney for a misdemeanor charge on 10/13/2023 and in just 4 months my case was DISMISSED 🥳

During that time, Rachel was efficient in contacting me and requesting all the necessary documents I needed to add to my file to ensure my case was dismissed (proof of anger management completion, proof of individualized counseling, proof of couples counseling, 3 letters of character)

A hearing date was scheduled for 1/10/24 which I didn’t have to attend and was rescheduled to 2/21/24. My case has been dismissed before this second hearing date.

Rachel has been easy to contact via text and email and would send me updates of my case via text in a timely manner. She has answered my questions throughly and has provided useful resources to assist me in this process.

I was set up to pay $4500 total, paying $500 a month over the next 9 months, but because my case was dismissed way earlier than I expected,  Rachel informed me that Mrs. Harrell would not be charging me any additional fees.  I ended paying a total of $2000.

I highly recommend Mrs. Harrell’s Law Office as they are efficient, dependable, responsive and most importantly, prioritize your case to provide you the best possible outcome.

Thank you Mrs. Harrell and Rachel!!!!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks so much for taking the time to write such a great and thorough review. So pleased we could get the dismissal.  And thanks for naming Rachel in your review. We appreciate her just as much you do. We wish you the very best.

reviews.text: I called recently and spoke to Rachel. She was extremely polite and gave me all the information I needed. Very pleased with my experience. Highly recommend and make sure you ask for Rachel.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks so much! We're lucky to have Rachel.

reviews.text: Very professional and proficient. I am satisfied with my case outcome.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks, Danesha! We strive for client satisfaction. Thanks for entrusting your case to us. We're worked hard to get that outcome.

reviews.text: Very good service. GREAT Experienced lawyer. Had my case dismissed...would highly recommend to anyone.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thanks, Billy! Glad we got your case DISMISSED.

reviews.text: My husband had hit himself in the head and said I did it and I was charged with DV family violence, Samantha was my lawyer and then Ms. Harrell was the one that finalized the deal I never once had to appear in the courthouse or at the proceedings I communicated in the beginning that I would not be taking a plea deal and only accepting it as dismissed and it was dismissed. It took close to a year but it was worth the wait and I don’t believe with a public defender I would have gotten it dismissed.. this law office truly fights for you!!! Highly highly highly recommend!!!!!
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Rebekah, thanks very much for the review. We are so pleased that we were able to get your case dismissed. We wish the very best in the new year!!

Hi Robert. I have never represented you or a family member or a friend, so I'm asking you to remove your post. It appears you have me confused with another lawyer. So please remove your post from my page. I wish you a Happy Holiday and wonderful new year.

reviews.text: If there were 10 stars I would rate them at that. Mrs.Harrell was very professional while handling my cases which included 2x second degree felonies 2x class A misdemeanors and a motion to revoke probation which all ended in the best expected outcome.She answered all my questions and was very knowledgeable of all the process. I’ve used Ms.Harrell’s service many of times over the years and it has always been an exceptional experience. Her paralegal Teresa has been phenomenal in my cases and provided updates every step of the way. I Definitely would recommend their services to family and friends. Ms.Harrell is the definition of professionalism and will get the job done with a straight to the point approach. She is very experienced in her field and expressed true concern for me as a client over the years ,her court room presence is nothing less than a force of dominance to win the case. She will fight to the end for you as she fought for me. Thank you, Ms.Harrell and all of the associates at your law firm I am forever grateful for your services.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hey Earlie, Wow!  Thanks very much for those kind words. When your case was over, you walked out of court with your little boy - and nothing was more important than making sure you went home to be with him.  Wishing you and him the very best.
